executive_summary: The ABS is Australia's national statistical agency (a non-corporate Commonwealth entity in the Treasury portfolio), best understood as a scientific/data agency that is capital- and data-intensive and modernising under demand and response-rate pressure. The 2025-26 plan is dominated by two large, time-bound commitments: delivering the 2026 Census of Population and Housing, and the Big Data, Timely Insights (BDTI) program, which is rebuilding core statistical processing in a cloud-based analytics environment and delivering a complete monthly CPI from 26 November 2025. The agency is also reforming data acquisition toward a digital-first model in response to falling survey response rates, and leading the APS Data Profession to build data capability across government. These signals point to demand around program/delivery assurance, digital and data platform build, data integration, cyber/security assurance (especially for the Census), and workforce/capability uplift. The plan text supplied is high-level (a Statistician's message and section headings only), so several opportunities are inferred from named initiatives rather than detailed activity descriptions.

strategic_priorities.title: Deliver a secure, trusted 2026 Census
strategic_priorities.description: Finalise preparations through 2025-26 to run the 2026 Census of Population and Housing as an easy and secure digital experience for all Australians.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: In 2026 we'll conduct a secure and trusted Census of Population and Housing—an opportunity to tell Australia's story and guide future planning.

strategic_priorities.title: Big Data, Timely Insights (BDTI) modernisation
strategic_priorities.description: Rebuild core statistical tools and processes in a cloud-based data analytics environment, enabling a complete monthly CPI measure from 26 November 2025.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Our Big Data, Timely Insights (BDTI) program is rebuilding core statistical tools and processes in a cloud-based data analytics environment.

strategic_priorities.title: Modernise data acquisition / digital-first collection
strategic_priorities.description: Reform data sourcing to respond to the global long-term decline in survey response rates, creating simple, safe digital experiences and using administrative and alternative data sources.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are modernising our data acquisition processes to respond to the global long-term decline in survey response rates and to adopt a digital-first approach to data collection.

strategic_priorities.title: Build data integration capability and partnerships
strategic_priorities.description: Partner with government and the private sector to link public and private datasets for policy-relevant research and unlock new data sources.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: we aim to build data integration capability, linking public and private datasets for policy relevant research.

strategic_priorities.title: Lead the APS Data Profession
strategic_priorities.description: Attract, develop and retain data capabilities of ABS staff and the broader APS by leading the APS Data Profession.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We will continue to lead the Australian Public Service (APS) Data Profession.

reform_and_change_drivers: 2026 Census delivery deadline — a once-in-five-years national operation with security and public-trust expectations.
Complete monthly CPI launch on 26 November 2025 — a hard, externally visible delivery date dependent on BDTI cloud platform readiness.
Global long-term decline in survey response rates forcing a shift to administrative and alternative data sources.
Cloud migration and modernisation of legacy statistical processing systems under BDTI.
Growth in demand for timelier, more granular and integrated data from governments, communities and business.
APS-wide data capability uplift mandate as lead of the APS Data Profession.
challenges_and_risks: Security and public trust around the 2026 Census, given high public sensitivity to data handling and the legacy of prior Census IT incidents.
Delivery risk on BDTI and the monthly CPI go-live — fixed date, cloud re-platforming of core economic statistics.
Declining survey response rates undermining the quality of traditional collections.
Data integration involving linked public/private datasets raises privacy, governance and security exposure.
Workforce and data-capability shortages across the APS that the ABS is tasked to address.
Modernising legacy systems while maintaining continuity of critical economic and social statistics.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Run a secure, trusted 2026 Census with no repeat of past delivery/IT failures; finalise preparations during 2025-26.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent gateway-style delivery assurance and readiness health checks across Census preparation milestones (digital collection, processing, contact-centre and field operations), with go/no-go assurance ahead of the 2026 enumeration.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The Census is named as the marquee 2025-26 activity with explicit emphasis on 'secure and trusted' delivery. A fixed national deadline plus high public-trust stakes make independent assurance a credible, time-bound need.

opportunity_areas.area: Census cyber and data security assurance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Ensure the Census digital experience and supporting systems are secure given acute public sensitivity to data protection.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Security assessment and assurance of the online Census collection platform and supporting data systems — penetration-style assessment, security architecture review, and independent assurance of controls ahead of go-live.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan repeatedly stresses 'secure' Census and digital collection. Given the 2016 eCensus precedent (public memory of an outage), independent security assurance is a strong fit.

opportunity_areas.area: BDTI / monthly CPI platform build and delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Rebuild core statistical tools and processes in a cloud-based analytics environment and deliver complete monthly CPI from 26 Nov 2025.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Systems development, integration and process-analysis support for the cloud-based statistical processing pipeline, plus service transition and support/operations as components go live.
opportunity_areas.rationale: BDTI is explicitly described as rebuilding core processes in a cloud data analytics environment with a hard CPI delivery date — clear digital/data engineering demand. The firm can build and implement, not just advise.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: De-risk a multi-component, fixed-deadline modernisation of core economic statistics.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Delivery assurance / embedded PMO and benefits-management support for the BDTI program, with independent stage-gate reviews around the monthly CPI and subsequent statistical migrations.
opportunity_areas.rationale: BDTI is a named multi-year transformation of mission-critical systems with an externally visible launch date — exactly the profile that sustains program delivery and assurance support.

opportunity_areas.area: Data integration capability and information strategy
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Build capability to link public and private datasets for policy-relevant research and unlock alternative data sources.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Information strategy and data-integration capability uplift — data management frameworks, linkage methodology support, and analytics tooling to operationalise administrative/alternative data sources.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names data integration and use of administrative/alternative data as deliberate priorities driven by declining survey response rates.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Create simple, safe, user-friendly digital experiences to replace survey-based collection.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: User experience and service design discovery for digital-first respondent journeys, plus business/process analysis to redesign data acquisition workflows.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly commits to 'simple, safe and user-friendly digital experiences' and a digital-first collection approach.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Attract, develop and retain data capabilities across the ABS and the wider APS as lead of the APS Data Profession.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Data capability framework and workforce planning, with training and learning pathways to build APS-wide data skills.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Leading the APS Data Profession to 'attract, develop and retain' data capability is a stated priority and a natural workforce/capability engagement.

opportunity_areas.area: Privacy and probity for linked-data partnerships
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Manage governance and integrity exposure when linking public and private datasets and procuring data partnerships.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Probity advisory (non-legal) and contract/commercial advice for data-sharing arrangements and acquisition of alternative data sources.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Building partnerships and unlocking new data sources implies procurement and probity activity, though the plan does not detail specific approaches to market.

recommended_actions.action: Request a meeting with the ABS Census program leadership and the SES owner of 2026 Census delivery to position independent delivery and security assurance ahead of enumeration.
recommended_actions.rationale: The Census is the highest-stakes, most time-bound activity in the plan; readiness assurance must be procured well before the 2026 field operation, so the 2025-26 window is the decision point.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the BDTI program leadership / CDO function with a capability statement covering cloud statistical-pipeline build, integration and delivery assurance, referencing the 26 November 2025 monthly CPI go-live.
recommended_actions.rationale: A fixed, externally visible CPI delivery date concentrates delivery and assurance demand now; aligning to that milestone makes the pitch concrete.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short thought piece on secure digital-first data collection and respondent UX, and share with the data acquisition modernisation branch.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan signals a sustained shift to digital-first collection and administrative data; an evidence-led point of view opens a credible conversation on service design and data integration.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor AusTender and Treasury-portfolio Senate Estimates for Census and BDTI procurements and cost/schedule commentary.
recommended_actions.rationale: External research was unavailable this round; tracking procurement and Estimates will surface timing of approaches to market and any emerging delivery pressure.

engagement_targets: SES owner / First Assistant Statistician responsible for the 2026 Census program
BDTI program lead and the ABS Chief Data Officer / statistical infrastructure group
Chief Information Officer / Chief Information Security Officer (Census and BDTI security)
Head of data acquisition / collection modernisation
APS Data Profession lead within the ABS (workforce capability)
Chief Operating Officer / CFO and the Audit and Risk Committee (assurance and governance)
talking_points: The 2026 Census is a fixed-deadline, high-trust national operation — independent gateway-style delivery and security assurance reduces the risk of a repeat of past Census IT incidents.
BDTI's monthly CPI go-live on 26 November 2025 is an externally visible commitment; we can support cloud pipeline build, integration and stage-gate assurance to protect the date.

Declining survey response rates make the digital-first collection and administrative-data shift mission-critical — we bring service design and data-integration capability to make that transition safe and usable.
As lead of the APS Data Profession, the ABS has a capability mandate we can support with workforce planning and training pathways.
We build as well as advise — relevant where BDTI and digital collection need systems development and integration, not just review.
caveats: The supplied plan text is essentially the Australian Statistician's message plus section headings, not the detailed key-activities, risk-oversight or performance-measure content. Opportunities are therefore anchored to clearly named initiatives (Census 2026, BDTI/monthly CPI, digital-first collection, data integration, APS Data Profession) but the specific scope, budgets and procurement timing are inferred. External research returned no verified results due to a tool limitation, so news, ANAO and parliamentary sections are empty and should be refreshed before engagement. Cyber and security-assurance opportunities assume the firm's non-legal assessment scope and would need to fit ABS clearance and secrecy requirements.
